data.title: Aker Security collaborates with Cyviz to create control rooms for real-time data analysis; services cover thousands of employees.
data.description: In Norway, Aker Security manages security for a significant portion of their Group, which includes thousands of employees. Their services encompass travel security, global event monitoring, crisis management, and security advisory services. A key issue faced in the current security environment is effectively handling vast amounts of data in time-sensitive and coordinated scenarios. Due to this challenge, they sought assistance from Cyviz to aggregate, visualize, and utilize data efficiently.

data.title: European firms adopting managed services for networks, integrating AI technology. ISG reports the shift in approach.
data.description: In Europe, businesses are adopting managed services integrating AI-assisted and AI-driven features to transform their network strategies, according to ISG. This shift indicates a significant change in how companies are managing their networks.

data.title: Natural catastrophe losses reached over $100 billion in 2025, lower by $40 billion from 2024, warning against a false sense of security.
data.description: In London on January 29, 2026, it was reported that in 2025, natural catastrophes led to over US$100 billion in insured losses, marking the sixth year in a row surpassing this amount. This total saw a decrease of $40 billion compared to the previous year. Notably, these losses were incurred even without any hurricanes striking the United States. This data underscores the ongoing severity and durability of natural catastrophe risk.

data.description: A prominent European company, Faculty, has introduced Faculty Frontier™, a platform that aids enterprises in implementing Decision Intelligence effectively. This innovative platform seamlessly integrates with current technologies and data sources to provide comprehensive insights and recommendations that contribute to business success. The launch of Faculty Frontier™ occurred in London on January 29, 2026, showcasing its potential to drive business value through connected insights and recommendations.

data.title: Coinbase CEO matches $1,000 for kids in TrumpAccounts, supports financial literacy. Will contribute in Bitcoin.
data.description: Brian Armstrong, the CEO of Coinbase Global Inc. (NASDAQ: COIN), announced that the company has joined the TrumpAccounts initiative by pledging to match the $1,000 contribution for eligible children of employees. Armstrong expressed his support for the federal government initiative, highlighting the significance of early investments in financial security and literacy for children. He stated that Coinbase is proud to participate in matching the $1,000 from the U.S. Treasury for eligible children of Coinbase employees. Armstrong also mentioned the possibility of making the contribution in Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), emphasizing the importance of starting to invest early. The initiative aims to promote financial security and literacy for children, with Coinbase aligning itself with the program. Full details can be found on Benzinga.com.

data.title: TAL Education Group posted strong Q3 results with net revenues of $770.2M (+27%), net income at $130.6M (+466%), & cash of $3,617.4M.
data.description: TAL Education Group, a smart learning provider in China, reported its unaudited financial results for the third quarter of fiscal year 2026. In this period, net revenues were US$770.2 million, up 27.0% from the same period the year before, with a reported income from operations of US$93.1 million. The non-GAAP income from operations, excluding share-based compensation expenses, was US$104.0 million. Net income attributable to TAL was US$130.6 million, while non-GAAP net income attributable to TAL reached US$141.4 million. For the first nine months ended November 30, 2025, net revenues were US$2,206.5 million, with income from operations of US$203.6 million. Non-GAAP net income attributable to TAL was US$319.3 million for this period. TAL's cash, cash equivalents, and short-term investments totaled US$3,617.4 million as of November 30, 2025. TAL's financial performance showed growth, with a focus on integrating technology into learning experiences and enhancing content, products, and services. TAL's strategic initiatives aim to build competitive advantages and generate value for users and society.

data.title: New study by BCG, Heidrick & INSEAD shows boards in emerging markets facing higher uncertainty, improving governance.
data.description: A recent worldwide research project delves into the ways in which boards in developing economies are enhancing governance, resilience, and decision-making in the face of increasing uncertainty and complexity. The collaboration between Boston Consulting Group (BCG) and Heidrick & Struggles, a top-notch provider, has produced insightful findings. The study was released on January 29, 2026, in London. The report sheds light on how boards are adapting to challenges in the current landscape.

data.title: Apollo refinances Pan-European logistics and industrial portfolio with €900M for Cerberus and Arrow Capital Partners.
data.description: A customized and secure senior financial solution has been implemented to refinance a high-quality portfolio, aimed at consolidating the base of lenders involved. This initiative is focused on improving the financial position by addressing the outstanding debt related to the portfolio. The refinancing operation involves significant capital, positioning it as a strategic move to enhance the overall stability and effectiveness of the financial structure. The refinancing action is expected to have a substantial impact on the financial outlook and strengthen the relationship with lenders.
